heroTitle: Test real native Tamil clips inside the lesson flow.
heroText: This localhost version rebuilds the lesson experience around real open-licensed Tamil corpus audio. The clips are authentic, but they are not exact matches for the original home-conversation phrases.
panelList: Real OpenSLR Tamil audio instead of browser-generated speech
How native read-speech clips feel inside the same lesson UI
Whether authenticity improves the learning experience even when the lines are not perfect home-dialogue matches
title: Open-license native clip test
description: This is the same app flow rebuilt around real OpenSLR SLR65 clips. The tradeoff is important: the audio is genuinely native Tamil, but the corpus is not a conversational home-Tamil library, so some lines feel more formal or less targeted than the original lesson.
dialect: Tamil corpus audio from native speakers

audio.note: This lesson uses real open-licensed WAV files extracted from OpenSLR SLR65. It is meant to test whether authentic native audio improves the experience, not to represent the final curriculum.

source.title: OpenSLR SLR65 source
source.summary: Audio clips in this lesson were extracted from the official OpenSLR SLR65 Tamil corpus and kept as original WAV files for local testing.
source.attribution: Source corpus: OpenSLR SLR65, 'Crowdsourced high-quality Tamil multi-speaker speech data set', copyright 2018-2019 Google, Inc.
source.notes: These are native-speaker recordings, but the corpus is mostly read sentences rather than a curated conversational Chennai-family lesson. Use this page to judge authenticity and fit, not as the final product.

phrases.meaning: Soon I will learn to speak Tamil well.
phrases.useCase: Useful as a motivation line for a learner testing the app.
phrases.note: This is one of the closest corpus lines to the app's actual purpose, even though it is not part of the original home lesson.

phrases.useCase: An example of a simple personal update that feels more natural than news-style Tamil.
phrases.note: This clip is more usable for a learner app than many of the corpus's news-like sentences.

phrases.meaning: Don't hide anything from your parents.
phrases.useCase: Relevant to the family theme, even though it is still more like a statement than a conversation turn.
phrases.note: This line helps test whether family-related corpus audio feels more relevant to the app's audience.

phrases.useCase: Useful for hearing how a clean full-sentence response sounds in real native audio.
phrases.note: This is not as warm as the original home lesson, but it gives us a clear native clip with natural pacing.

phrases.transliteration: ennada idhu, namma time sari illaiyennu ninaichen
phrases.script: என்னடா இது நம்ம டைம் சரி இல்லையேன்னு நினைச்சேன்
phrases.meaning: What is this, I thought our time wasn't going well.
phrases.useCase: This is one of the more colloquial-sounding clips in the corpus and useful for testing spoken feel.
phrases.note: The wording is much more colloquial than most of the dataset, which makes it especially valuable for this experiment.

phrases.meaning: There will be happiness in the family.
phrases.useCase: Relevant as a family-oriented line for checking whether the emotional tone of native audio helps the experience.
phrases.note: This is not a direct conversational reply, but it keeps the experiment tied to family vocabulary.

dialogues.prompt: You want a learner-facing line that actually mentions speaking Tamil.
dialogues.context: Which clip is the most on-brand for a Tamil learning app experiment?
dialogues.explanation: This is the most directly relevant line in the corpus for a Tamil learning context.

dialogues.prompt: You want to test whether a family-oriented corpus clip feels usable in the app.
dialogues.context: Which line is most clearly tied to parents or family?
dialogues.explanation: This line is the clearest family-related example in the extracted open-license set.
answers: ennada idhu, namma time sari illaiyennu ninaichen

dialogues.prompt: You want to hear the most casual-sounding reaction clip in the set.
dialogues.context: Which answer best fits that goal?
dialogues.explanation: This line is more colloquial than the others and helps test spoken feel better than the more formal clips.
answers: ennada idhu, namma time sari illaiyennu ninaichen

recalls.prompt: Say: 'Soon I will learn to speak Tamil well.'
recalls.note: This is the strongest mission-aligned clip in the extracted dataset set.
A simple everyday statement with real corpus audio.

recalls.prompt: Say: 'Don't hide anything from your parents.'
recalls.note: This keeps the experiment connected to family vocabulary.
Good for hearing clean sentence pacing in a native clip.
